author: @Vidyut_Gore
text: You could add ice cubes to speed up the process. We do that when mixing lye to make soap. The solution cools much faster with ice cubes. The heat melts the cubes, which keep adding water slowly and keep the solution cool.

author: @gebitom
text: Hi Robert! Thanks for sharing! Great experiment! I have some suggestions for improvement. I had to do it 2 times.  The first time I did it the same as you with 5g of fine mesh graphene. I put everything into snow to cool but anyway the reaction run away. Massive violet and brown fuming and even carbon flakes flew out. The permanganate decomposed due to the heat and nearly no graphite was oxidized, no color change. I dumped it because after 30 minutes everything was still deep black. Maybe the reaction was so massive due the fine mesh graphene. The second time I cooled the sulfuric acid down to 0°C inside the main container. I mixed the graphebe into it. Stirring it for 5 Minutes and than pour slowly the permanganate into. No fuming at all!! It changed soon to a very bright greenish. Nice. Than I moved it into a waterbath and heated it up to 40°C and some degree more until I noticed the smell of ozone. Very slow and controlled reaction! Then I moved it out from the bath until the ozone stopped bubbling up. Keep stiring. Move back to the bath until the O3 bubbling appeares again and so on. Keep stiring until the O3 bubbling stops and the slurry becomes yellowish/brown. Than move everything into an icewater bath. Poor slowly the 4°C 400g DI water into it. Monitor the temperature. Wondefull color change! You see the golden flakes swimming in the solution. Magnificent! The slurry was sattled after 5. I will repeat deluting it before washing it with HCl. Hope I was able to give some positive feedback. Good luck Thomas.

author: @nigeljohnson9820
text:
I have very recently discovered the changes n UK law related to private use of chemicals. New regulations relating to explosive prer
Precursors and poisons has severely restricted private access to chemicals, with access to once common household chemicals now illegal. Certainly access to strong acid has recently changed, including strong hydrochloric acid. The other common acids falling into both the explosive precursor and poison category. Other materials covered by the new legislation is a number of oxidizing agents. While some of these chemicals are still available on web sites such as Amazon or eBay, there purchase is illegal.
It is possible to apply for a home office license to buy some of these chemicals for a specific purpose, I have the impression that the home office will not grant a license for chemical experimentations, which they frown upon outside an industrial or research environment.  From what I have read in the last few days, application for a chemical use license is not a simple process.
I would be interested in your comments about this subject and the application for a home office EPP license. 
While some batteries can be made with household chemicals. I am reasonable sure that the chemicals shown in this video are now illegal. As an industrial user and a qualified chemist, I believe that you are in a different legal position to say an electronic engineer, regarding access to these chemicals.

author: @jongboon
text:
Dear Robert,

Thanks for sharing your fantastic videos. I am trying to obtain a stable dispersion or homogeneous mixture 0.1 wt% of graphene oxide with hydrocarbon such as diesel but couldnt get it. I am wondering if there is any way to make this happen? I would highly appreciate your advice. Thanks.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: graphene oxide disperses well in N,N-dimethylformamide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, tetrahydrofuran, and ethylene glycol or cosolvents of DMF with water in a 9:1 ratio by bath sonication - this will help you reduce it to graphene that can the be dispersed in organic solvents DMF, DMSO, THF, NMP, acetonitrile, acetone, ethanol, diethyl-ether, toluene, and DCB, and probably diesel - though i have never tried to disperse in diesel myself

author: @joeglory425
text: It occurred to me that I should avoid excessively high temperatures during the process of making graphene oxide because those same high temperatures would convert the graphene oxide back to graphene.  So what I did to moderate temperatures was I put a bunch of glass marbles in with my reactants, the idea being the excess mass would absorb the energy of the reaction and keep temperatures a bit lower.  I'm pleased with the results but I don't have any hard data to back it up.  All of this was in addition to putting my reactants in the freezer so they would be very cold to start off.  I did not finish off with a high temperature stage in the oven.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @Pelukeitor
text: Robert think that this well translated?

author: @Pelukeitor
text:
Preparación de Oxido de Grafeno a Larga Escala

 permanganato de potasio ( 15 g) 
 grafito expandido( 5 g ) 
ácido sulfúrico concentrado ( 98 % , 100 ml)
agua ( 500 ml ) y hielo


Inicialmente se agita el grafito expandido y el permanganato de potasio hasta homogeneizarse .
Luego meter en otro recipiente,(recipiente con agua y hielo).

Se le añade ácido sulfúrico concentrado con agitación continua (produce humo al principio).

A continuación, se retira el baño de agua y hielo . Agitación continua hasta quedar como una mousse de chocolate.
( alrededor de 30 min ) a temperatura ambiente con una gran expansión volumétrica .

Se añade agua desionizada 400 ml (poco a poco ) , y se agita rápidamente para evitar que se vuelva efervescente . A continuación,
el recipiente se le agrega agua 90 ℃ , y después de 1 hora se obtiene una suspensión homogénea que se
pone de color amarillo oscuro . La suspensión se filtra a continuación y se somete a lavado por dos veces
y se centrifuga ( 10.000 rpm , 2 h por ciclo ) para eliminar las impurezas .

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Gracias por esto, pero si se produce mucho humo. Hey un error. Normalmente, no produce mucho humo. 'una mousse de chocolate' - que buono lol - lo me gusta.

text: I shat myself laughing when you walked out with that on LOL!!! They got to you too?! I wear a helmet..  always.. just incase!! OH MAN! lol... Hey check out my rectenna.. please make it, i think it works good.sooo easy to make!.. :D I need opinions.. your funny!@ LOL Did you try mixing alum with the lead oxide stuff. for the "lead wafers" :D . i wasnt sure if it would mix well...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Hiya mate - I checked out the rectenna - like it - i don't have much time at the moment to give it the proper attention but i have added it to my to look at soon list lol - very interesting stuff. I haven't tried mixing in the alum either - not enough time in the day lol

replies.author: @richharrisonline
replies.text: No probs just wondered if you seen one before.. put it on yur backburner :) I know what you mean.. so much stuff to do, not enough seconds alotted to us it seems :(
